behavior_summary: U8P_CUP::look is the object's 'U8P_CUP::look' (examine) handler. When the player inspects the CUP object it displays a single short text/bark: "cup ".
After emitting the bark the handler yields/ends by calling suspend (returns control to the engine).
There is no branching, menu interaction, flag read/write, or other side effects recorded; the handler is purely descriptive.
notable_control_flow: Single linear flow: on entry (U8P_CUP::look) -> emit bark "cup " -> call suspend to yield/terminate the process.
No conditional branches, loops, or calls to other internal functions are present.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "cup " intentional (formatting artifact) or meaningful in the game UI?
The raw metadata included two 'Unknown' nodes ("{" and a raw 'suspend' node). Are there any context-specific side effects of the suspend call beyond ending the U8P_CUP::look handler?

main_function.name: U8P_CUP::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short descriptive text when the player examines the CUP object and then yield/terminate the U8P_CUP::look handler.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player uses the U8P_CUP::look/examine action on the CUP object (process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_CUP::look
function_map.role: U8P_CUP::look/examine handler that emits a single bark and ends

external_calls.purpose: Yield or terminate the U8P_CUP::look process and return control to the engine (end the handler).
